findings.title: Build User Behavior Tracking Pipeline
findings.description: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user. The pipeline must handle at minimum 50k events/second at peak and store raw events with full user identifiers for profile enrichment downstream.
assumptions: The current infrastructure can support the new data pipeline.
recommendations: Ensure compliance with GDPR during data collection.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user is authenticated, WHEN they browse content, THEN their browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics are captured and streamed to the data lake in near real-time.

findings.title: Implement ML Recommendation Engine
findings.description: Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data to serve personalized content suggestions. The model should incorporate collaborative filtering and content-based approaches, targeting a 20% improvement in content CTR within 60 days.
assumptions: The recommendation engine can effectively utilize the user profile data.
recommendations: Conduct A/B testing to validate the effectiveness of the recommendation engine.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN user profile data is available, WHEN the recommendation engine processes this data, THEN it should serve personalized content suggestions that improve content CTR by at least 20% within 60 days.

findings.title: Create User Preference Dashboard
findings.description: Build a user-facing dashboard that displays what behavioral data has been collected about them, including inferred interests and engagement patterns. Include toggles to opt out of specific tracking categories.
assumptions: Users will engage with the dashboard to manage their preferences.
recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user accesses their dashboard, WHEN they view their data, THEN they should see their inferred interests, content categories, and engagement patterns along with options to opt out of specific tracking categories.

findings.title: Add Cross-Device Tracking
findings.description: Link user activity across mobile app and web browser sessions using deterministic and probabilistic matching to enable a unified user profile regardless of device.
assumptions: The cross-device identity graph can be accurately maintained.
recommendations: Implement clear opt-out options for users.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is logged in on multiple devices, WHEN they interact with content, THEN their activity should be linked across devices to maintain a unified user profile.

findings.title: Implement Data Export for Advertisers
findings.description: Create an API and batch export system that shares anonymized user profile segments with advertising partners while ensuring compliance with GDPR.
assumptions: Anonymization processes will effectively prevent re-identification.
recommendations: Conduct a legal review of the anonymization pipeline.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN user profile segments are ready for export, WHEN the API is called, THEN it should return anonymized cohort-level data without risking individual re-identification.

findings.title: Conduct GDPR Compliance Audit
findings.description: Perform a full GDPR compliance audit of the PersonaLens profiling system to address legal concerns regarding data processing and consent mechanisms.
assumptions: Legal requirements will be clearly defined and actionable.
recommendations: Address all flagged concerns before product launch.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the audit team is assembled, WHEN they review the profiling system, THEN they should identify all areas of non-compliance and provide actionable recommendations.

findings.title: Create Transparency Dashboard
findings.description: Develop a transparency dashboard that shows users what data is collected about them, enhancing user trust and engagement.
assumptions: Users will utilize the transparency dashboard to understand data collection.
recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is intuitive and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user accesses the transparency dashboard, WHEN they view their data, THEN they should see a clear representation of what data is collected and how it is used.

findings.title: Implement Granular Consent Mechanisms
findings.description: Develop granular consent mechanisms for data processing, particularly for cross-device tracking and data sharing with advertisers, to comply with GDPR.
assumptions: Users will appreciate having control over their data.
recommendations: Conduct user testing to ensure the consent mechanisms are clear and effective.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user is presented with consent options, WHEN they select their preferences, THEN their choices should be accurately reflected in the data processing activities.

findings.title: Monitor Privacy Opt-Out Rate
findings.description: Implement monitoring for the Privacy Opt-Out Rate to ensure compliance with privacy regulations and maintain user trust.
assumptions: Monitoring tools will be effective in tracking opt-out rates.
recommendations: Set up alerts for significant changes in opt-out rates.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place, WHEN users opt out of data tracking, THEN the opt-out rate should be accurately recorded and reported.

findings.title: Conduct User Feedback Collection
findings.description: Implement a system for collecting user feedback on the personalization features to continuously improve the user experience.
assumptions: Users will provide valuable feedback on their experiences.
recommendations: Incorporate feedback mechanisms into the user interface.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback collection system is active, WHEN users interact with personalization features, THEN they should be prompted to provide feedback on their experience.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics findings has led to the creation of a comprehensive product backlog for the PersonaLens User Profiling project. Key requirements focus on building a robust user behavior tracking pipeline, implementing a machine learning recommendation engine, and ensuring compliance with GDPR through granular consent mechanisms and a transparency dashboard. The backlog includes a mix of critical, high, and medium priority items, with a clear focus on enhancing user trust and engagement while addressing legal and privacy concerns.
